11.reason: [Using `gets()` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Using `gets()` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Using `gets()` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers. Use `fgets()` instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and may cause compilation errors with modern compilers. Use `fgets` instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; may cause compilation error with modern compilers that remove `gets` from stdio.h]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it may cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments, `gets` is not available, leading to compilation errors.]

77.reason: [Incorrect loop condition: `a[i]!='\0'` assumes `a` is a null-terminated string, but `a` is an array of `long long int`; this leads to undefined behavior and incorrect summation]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect loop condition: `a[i]!='\0'` assumes `a` is a null-terminated string, but `a` is an array of `long long int`; this leads to undefined behavior and incorrect summation]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The array `a` is not null-terminated; comparing `a[i] != '\0'` is invalid because `a` contains `long long int` values, not characters. This leads to undefined behavior and incorrect summation.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [The array `a` is not null-terminated; comparing `a[i] != '\0'` is invalid because `a` contains `long long int` values, not characters. This leads to undefined behavior and incorrect summation.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect loop condition: `a[i]` is a `long long int`, not a char; comparing to `'\0'` is meaningless and leads to undefined behavior. Should iterate up to `p+1` instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [The array `a` is of type `long long int` and is not null-terminated. Comparing `a[i] != '\0'` is incorrect because numeric arrays don't use null terminators like strings. This leads to reading uninitialized memory and undefined behavior.]
